signals.title: USD/JPY — Hawkish Fed + Intervention Risk + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.504 — the pair is trading above the 160.00 level, which has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. Intervention risk is elevated on a sustained break higher. The hawkish Fed surprise (June FOMC) and strong US data (USD CESI net +30) are driving dollar strength, while the 265bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry premium (SOFR 3.63% vs TONA 0.98%) continues to support the pair. JPY COT LF net short −115,036 (week ending Jun 9) indicates crowded spec short positioning, increasing volatility risk around the 160.00 level. Watch for BoJ commentary or a move towards 162.00 to confirm intervention intent.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail Extreme
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −22,354 (specs fading a hawkish CB) vs RBNZ hike 40% priced for 8 Jul. If RBNZ delivers or signals a hike, short-squeeze risk is elevated — crowded spec short unwind targets 0.5800+. NZD/USD at 0.5715. Retail sentiment shows 94% long, an extreme contrarian signal, suggesting retail is fading the institutional short. This divergence amplifies potential volatility. Watch for RBNZ forward guidance at the next meeting.

signals.title: GBP/NZD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: GBP is the strongest G10 currency this week, averaging +0.545% vs peers, while NZD is the weakest at −0.487%. This creates a 1.032% leader-to-laggard spread, indicating a strong bilateral theme. The GBP/NZD cross is supported by a significant 150bp carry premium (BoE 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25%) favoring GBP. This divergence is driven by relative central bank stances and G10 performance. Watch for RBNZ's next meeting on 8 Jul for potential shifts in carry dynamics.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + CESI Weakness
signals.text: AUD/USD IV 13.4% vs HV30 7.8% — a +5.6% VRP indicates elevated hedging demand for downside protection, significantly above realized volatility. This suggests market participants are pricing in higher future volatility despite current price action. AUD CESI net −8 misses (moderate negative bias) points to disappointing data, which could fuel further AUD weakness. Watch for upcoming Australian employment data to confirm the CESI trend.
